nodes.context: Sunni Islamist paramilitary group fighting in Syrian civil war, later renamed Jabhat Fatah Al-Sham.
Global jihadist network; Al-Nusra was its Syrian affiliate until 2016.

Al-Qaeda loyalist group formed in 2018 as anti-HTS front.

Moderate wing of Syrian rebel forces focused on ending Assad family reign.
Al-Qaeda affiliate in Iraq, later merged into ISIL.

nodes.original_text_name: Islamic State of Iraq and Al-Sham

Global jihadist network; Al-Nusra was its Syrian affiliate.
Islamist group that cooperated with Al-Nusra in attacking a Syrian air defense base.
Islamist rebel group; its commander was killed, leading to tensions with ISIL.
Sunni Islamist paramilitary group fighting in Syrian civil war, aiming to establish an Islamic state.
Secular and pro-democratic rebel group in Syrian civil war.
Global jihadist organization, Al-Nusra's former affiliate.

edges.evidence_quote: In April 2013, Al-Nusra Front was publicly confirmed as the official Syrian affiliate of Al-Qaeda
edges.context: Al-Nusra was the Syrian affiliate of Al-Qaeda from 2013 to 2016.

edges.evidence_quote: In July 2016, Al-Nusra announced that it was breaking ties with Al-Qaeda
edges.context: Al-Nusra broke ties with Al-Qaeda in 2016.

edges.target: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant
edges.evidence_quote: The tactics of Al-Nusra Front differed markedly from those of its rival jihadist group ISIL
edges.context: Al-Nusra and ISIL were rival jihadist groups.

edges.evidence_quote: While secular and pro-democratic rebel groups of the Syrian Revolution such as the Free Syrian Army were focused on ending the decades-long reign of the Assad family, Al-Nusra Front also sought the unification of Islamist forces
edges.context: Al-Nusra and Free Syrian Army had different goals but both fought Assad.

edges.evidence_quote: In March 2015, the militia joined other Syrian Islamist groups to form a joint command center called the Army of Conquest
edges.context: Al-Nusra was a founding member of the Army of Conquest coalition.

edges.evidence_quote: Jabhat Fatah Al-Sham (JFS) merged with four other groups to form Hayat Tahrir Al-Sham (HTS)
edges.context: JFS merged into HTS in 2017.

edges.evidence_quote: Mutual hostility progressively deteriorated into violent confrontations, with Al-Nusra commander Sami al-Oraydi accusing HTS of adopting nationalist doctrines
edges.context: After the merger, Al-Nusra loyalists clashed with HTS.

edges.evidence_quote: Sami Al-Oraydi, alongside other Al-Qaeda loyalists ... mobilised Al-Qaeda personnel in northwestern Syria to establish an anti-HTS front in the region, eventually forming Hurras Al-Din
edges.context: Al-Nusra loyalists formed Hurras Al-Din to fight HTS.

edges.evidence_quote: following violent clashes with Ahrar Al-Sham and other rebel groups
edges.context: Al-Nusra clashed with Ahrar Al-Sham in 2017.

edges.evidence_quote: Al-Nusra Front leader Julani stated that 'We are only here to accomplish one mission, to fight the regime and its agents on the ground, including Hezbollah and others'
edges.context: Al-Nusra considered Hezbollah an enemy and fought them.

edges.evidence_quote: In September 2015 Nusra absorbed Imam Bukhari Jamaat, an Uzbek group which is a part of Al-Qaeda
edges.context: Al-Nusra absorbed Imam Bukhari Jamaat in 2015.

edges.evidence_quote: Khorasan, also known as the Khorasan Group, is an alleged group of senior Al-Qaeda members who operate in Syria. The group has been reported to ... co-ordinate with Al-Nusra Front
edges.context: Khorasan Group coordinated with Al-Nusra.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Mujahideen Shura Council (2014–2015)
edges.context: Al-Nusra was allied with Mujahideen Shura Council from 2014 to 2015.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Jund al-Aqsa
edges.context: Al-Nusra was allied with Jund al-Aqsa.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Caucasus Emirate
edges.context: Al-Nusra was allied with Caucasus Emirate.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Turkistan Islamic Party
edges.context: Al-Nusra was allied with Turkistan Islamic Party.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Ajnad al-Kavkaz
edges.context: Al-Nusra was allied with Ajnad al-Kavkaz.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Malhama Tactical
edges.context: Al-Nusra was allied with Malhama Tactical.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Ashida'a Mujahideen Brigade
edges.context: Al-Nusra was allied with Ashida'a Mujahideen Brigade.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Ansar al-Din Front
edges.context: Al-Nusra was allied with Ansar al-Din Front.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Ansar al-Islam
edges.context: Al-Nusra was allied with Ansar al-Islam.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Islamic State
edges.context: Al-Nusra opposed the Islamic State.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... YPG
edges.context: Al-Nusra opposed the YPG.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... YPJ
edges.context: Al-Nusra opposed the YPJ.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... PKK
edges.context: Al-Nusra opposed the PKK.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Army of Revolutionaries
edges.context: Al-Nusra opposed the Army of Revolutionaries.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Kata'ib Hezbollah
edges.context: Al-Nusra opposed Kata'ib Hezbollah.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq
edges.context: Al-Nusra opposed Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Al-Abbas Brigade
edges.context: Al-Nusra opposed Al-Abbas Brigade.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Kata'ib Sayyid al-Shuhada
edges.context: Al-Nusra opposed Kata'ib Sayyid al-Shuhada.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Harakat Hezbollah al-Nujaba
edges.context: Al-Nusra opposed Harakat Hezbollah al-Nujaba.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Liwa Fatemiyoun
edges.context: Al-Nusra opposed Liwa Fatemiyoun.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Liwa Zainebiyoun
edges.context: Al-Nusra opposed Liwa Zainebiyoun.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Yarmouk Martyrs Brigade
edges.context: Al-Nusra opposed Yarmouk Martyrs Brigade.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Jaysh al-Jihad
edges.context: Al-Nusra opposed Jaysh al-Jihad.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Islamic Muthanna Movement
edges.context: Al-Nusra opposed Islamic Muthanna Movement.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Jaysh al-Islam (until 2016)
edges.context: Al-Nusra was allied with Jaysh al-Islam until 2016.

edges.evidence_quote: In August 2012, there were signs of Nusra cooperating with other rebels. The group took part in military operations with the Free Syrian Army (FSA).
edges.context: Al-Nusra cooperated with FSA in military operations against the Syrian government.

edges.evidence_quote: On 10 December 2012, the U.S. designated Nusra a foreign terrorist organization and an alias of Al-Qaeda in Iraq.
edges.context: The U.S. considered Al-Nusra an alias of Al-Qaeda in Iraq, indicating a structural link.

edges.target: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant
edges.evidence_quote: Open fighting between al-Nusra and ISIL (2013–2015)
edges.context: Al-Nusra and ISIL engaged in open armed conflict after a dispute over merger.

edges.evidence_quote: Al-Julani rejected the proposed merger and affirmed the group's allegiance to Al-Qaeda and its leader, Ayman Al-Zawahiri.
edges.context: Al-Nusra pledged allegiance to Al-Qaeda, making it the official Al-Qaeda branch in Syria.

edges.evidence_quote: In the air defence base assault they reportedly destroyed buildings and sabotaged radar and rockets after over-running the base in co-operation with Al-Fajr Islamic Movement and a group of Chechen fighters.
edges.context: Al-Nusra cooperated with Al-Fajr Islamic Movement in attacking a Syrian air defense base.

edges.evidence_quote: Al-Julani threatened to go to war with ISIL over their suspected role in the killing of senior Ahrar Al-Sham commander Abu Khaled Al-Souri.
edges.context: Al-Nusra defended Ahrar al-Sham by threatening ISIL after the killing of its commander.

edges.evidence_quote: In late October 2014, Al-Nusra began attacking some FSA and moderate Islamist groups that it was formerly allied with
edges.context: Al-Nusra attacked FSA after a period of alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: Al-Nusra effectively coordinated its operations with the FSA, moderate and conservative Syrian Islamists, and some independent jihadist factions.
edges.context: Al-Nusra and FSA coordinated operations in Idlib offensives.

edges.evidence_quote: Jabhat Al-Nusra was an official branch of Al-Qaeda.
edges.context: Al-Nusra was an official branch of Al-Qaeda until July 2016.

edges.evidence_quote: On 17 February 2016, nearly half of Jund Al-Aqsa, numbering 400 fighters, joined Al-Nusra.
edges.context: Jund Al-Aqsa fighters joined Al-Nusra.

edges.evidence_quote: On 9 October, Jund Al-Aqsa, after infighting between them and numerous rebel groups including Ahrar Al-Sham, merged with JFS.
edges.context: Jund Al-Aqsa merged with Jabhat Fatah Al-Sham.

edges.evidence_quote: Jaysh Al-Islam leader Zahran Alloush addressed Jabhat Al-Nusra as 'our brothers', saying 'We fight alongside them and they fight alongside us'.
edges.context: Jaysh Al-Islam fought alongside Al-Nusra.

edges.evidence_quote: The mid-2016 rebel offensive in Aleppo was carried out by Jabhat Fatah Al-Sham alongside the Turkistan Islamic Party and Ahrar Al-Sham.
edges.context: JFS fought alongside Ahrar Al-Sham in Aleppo offensive.

edges.evidence_quote: Ahrar Al-Sham, the Suqour Al-Sham Brigade, Jabhat Ahl Al-Sham, Jaysh Al-Islam and Fastaqim Union then established a joint operations room to combat Al-Nusra and its subfaction Jund Al-Aqsa.
edges.context: Ahrar Al-Sham formed operations room to combat Al-Nusra.

edges.evidence_quote: Condolences were issued to Jabhat Fatah Al-Sham over the killings, by the Uyghur jihadist Turkistan Islamic Party.
edges.context: Turkistan Islamic Party offered condolences to JFS, indicating alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: Al-Nusra offered bounties worth millions of dollars for the killing of Syrian President Bashar al-Assad and Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah.
edges.context: Al-Nusra targeted Hezbollah leader with bounty.

edges.evidence_quote: On 31 August, Hezbollah attacked a JFS outpost in the Qalamoun Mountains near the city of Arsal, Lebanon, on the Lebanon–Syria border, killing a number of their fighters.
edges.context: Hezbollah attacked JFS outpost.

edges.evidence_quote: The training camp had been operated by Al-Nusra Front and Al-Zenki since 2013.
edges.context: Al-Nusra and Al-Zenki jointly operated a training camp.

edges.evidence_quote: On 28 January 2017, several Salafi jihadist groups, including Jabhat Fatah Al-Sham, agreed upon a merger agreement, resulting in the formation of a new group called Hay'at Tahrir Al-Sham.
edges.context: Jabhat Fatah Al-Sham merged into Hay'at Tahrir Al-Sham.

edges.evidence_quote: The Islamic Jihad Union which is allied to Al-Qaeda.
edges.context: Islamic Jihad Union is allied to Al-Qaeda.

edges.evidence_quote: the people who were being supplied were Al-Nusra, and al Qaeda, and the extremist elements of jihadis coming from other parts of the world.
edges.context: Al-Nusra and Al-Qaeda were both recipients of weapons and funding from foreign states.

edges.evidence_quote: Saudi Arabia and Turkey were 'focusing their backing for the Syrian rebels on the combined Jaish Al-Fatah, or the Army of Conquest, a command structure for jihadist groups in Syria that [included] Jabhat Al-Nusra.'
edges.context: Al-Nusra was part of the Jaish Al-Fatah command structure.

edges.evidence_quote: Weapons have been passed on to Nusra by Ahrar ash-Sham according to a Nusra member and rebels.
edges.context: Ahrar ash-Sham supplied weapons to Al-Nusra.

edges.evidence_quote: a joint ISIS–al-Nusra operation along with 29 civilians, some of whom are known terrorists.
edges.context: ISIS and Al-Nusra conducted a joint operation to capture Lebanese soldiers and police.

edges.evidence_quote: Training is conducted in Aleppo by veteran jihadists who belong to the Khorasan Group, a collection of veteran Al-Qaeda operatives sent from Al-Qaeda strongholds along the Afghan-Pakistan border.
edges.context: Khorasan Group trained Al-Nusra's elite sniper unit.

edges.evidence_quote: the rebel Islamic Front and Al-Nusra Front groups infiltrated the industrial area of the town of Adra
edges.context: Islamic Front and Al-Nusra jointly attacked Adra.

edges.evidence_quote: Hay'at Tahrir Al-Sham wounded militants were receiving treatment in Israel.
edges.context: Hay'at Tahrir Al-Sham is the successor organization to Al-Nusra.

edges.evidence_quote: the Israeli Mossad assassinated the Syrian scientist, Aziz Azbar, with the assistance of Abu Amara Brigades, which is a faction within Hay'at Tahrir Al-Sham.
edges.context: Abu Amara Brigades is a faction within Hay'at Tahrir Al-Sham.
